volumes.title: Vol. I: The Bradford Fire (1980–1984)
volumes.description: Raw post-punk fury from the independent years. The manifesto.

volumes.title: Vol. II: The State of the Nation (1985–1987)
volumes.description: The EMI years. Political fire, banned from Top of the Pops.

volumes.title: Vol. III: Thunder (1989–1991)
volumes.description: The masterwork period. Folk, punk, and rock in perfect collision.

volumes.title: Vol. IV: The Wilderness (1993–1998)
volumes.description: EMI drops them. The music gets harder, then more reflective.

volumes.title: Vol. V: Ghosts and Carnivals (2000–2009)
volumes.description: Heaton dies. The band doesn't. Tribal rhythms, atmospheric landscapes.

volumes.title: Vol. VI: The Late Burning (2013–2024)
volumes.description: The renaissance. Critics finally catch up. The orchestra arrives.

volumes.title: Live Appendix: The Essential Live Recordings
volumes.description: For when you need to hear what the Family sounds like in a room together.
